name: dark-mode
context: Tailwind dark mode — media query, class toggle, data attribute strategies, and three-way theme switching
info: Tailwind's dark: variant lets agents style elements differently in dark mode. By default it uses the prefers-color-scheme media feature (OS preference), but can be configured for manual toggling via a CSS class or data attribute. This node covers all three strategies, the JavaScript needed for three-way toggling (light/dark/system), avoiding flash of unstyled content (FOUC), and persisting preference in localStorage. Agents need this knowledge when implementing theme switchers, adding dark mode support to existing components, or debugging dark mode not working. In v4, dark mode is customized via @custom-variant instead of the darkMode config key.
triggers: add dark mode to my app

sections.title: Media query strategy (default)
sections.body: Default behavior: `dark:` generates `@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark)` rules that follow the OS setting. No configuration needed. Works automatically on macOS, Windows, iOS, Android dark mode. Cannot be manually toggled by the user without switching OS settings.

sections.title: Class-based toggle
sections.body: v4: add `@custom-variant dark (&:where(.dark, .dark *));` to CSS. v3: set `darkMode: "class"` in config. Then add/remove the `dark` class on `<html>`. This gives full control — users can toggle manually. Persist choice in `localStorage`. Must include inline `<head>` script to prevent flash of wrong theme.

sections.title: Data attribute strategy
sections.body: Alternative to class: `@custom-variant dark (&:where([data-theme=dark], [data-theme=dark] *));`. Set `<html data-theme="dark">` to activate. Useful when integrating with frameworks that manage theme via data attributes (e.g., daisyUI, Radix Themes).

sections.title: Three-way toggle implementation
sections.body: Support light, dark, and system (OS default). Store "light" or "dark" in `localStorage.theme`. Remove the key for system mode. Inline detection script in `<head>`: check localStorage first, fall back to `window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)')`. Listen for `change` events on the matchMedia query to update when OS preference changes.

sections.title: Light/dark color pairing patterns
sections.body: Page bg: `bg-white dark:bg-gray-900`. Surface: `bg-gray-50 dark:bg-gray-800`. Primary text: `text-gray-900 dark:text-white`. Secondary: `text-gray-600 dark:text-gray-400`. Borders: `border-gray-200 dark:border-gray-700`. Actions: `bg-indigo-600 dark:bg-indigo-500`. Hover: `hover:bg-gray-50 dark:hover:bg-gray-700`. Shadows may need adjusted opacity: `shadow-sm dark:shadow-gray-900/20`.
